task_summary: Design a genome-wide CRISPR knockout screen targeting the entire human kinome: define the kinase gene set, select optimal SpCas9 sgRNAs, predict off-targets, add controls, and plan library construction + sequencing depth.

steps.title: Define the human kinome gene set
steps.description: Assemble a canonical human kinase gene list (HGNC symbols, Entrez IDs, kinase group/family — TK, TKL, STE, CK1, AGC, CAMK, CMGC, atypical, plus selected lipid/metabolic kinases) from a curated source (KinHub / Manning kinome / UniProt PKinase keyword). Produce kinome_genes.csv (~518-560 genes with group, family, UniProt, Entrez) and a figure kinome_groups.png showing gene counts per kinase group. State exactly which definition/scope is used.

steps.title: Acquire Brunello library and subset to kinases
steps.description: Download the Broad GPP Brunello human genome-wide CRISPR-KO library (76,441 sgRNAs, 19,114 genes, 4 guides/gene + 1,000 non-targeting controls; Doench 2016). Map kinome symbols to Brunello target genes (handle symbol aliases), subset to kinase guides, and report coverage: genes found, genes with <4 guides, genes missing entirely (candidates for de-novo design). Produce kinase_guides_brunello.csv and figure guides_per_gene.png.

steps.title: On-target quality assessment of selected guides
steps.description: For every selected guide compute/collect on-target quality metrics: Brunello Rule Set 2 (Azimuth) score, GC content, CDS position/percentile, poly-T (TTTT) terminator check, homopolymer runs, and BsmBI/Esp3I (CGTCTC) restriction-site conflicts for cloning. Flag low-quality guides. Produce guide_ontarget_qc.csv and figure ontarget_score_distributions.png (score + GC distributions).

steps.title: Genome-wide off-target prediction
steps.description: Install Cas-OFFinder (bioconda) and download the human genome (GRCh38) FASTA. Enumerate genome-wide near-matches for all selected 20-mer+NGG guides allowing up to 3-4 mismatches, then compute CFD off-target and MIT specificity scores per guide, counting 0/1/2/3-mismatch sites and exonic/on-gene hits. Flag guides with high-risk off-targets (high-CFD hits in coding regions). Produce offtarget_report.csv and figure offtarget_specificity.png. (Heaviest step — genome download + alignment.)

steps.title: Guide reselection and controls
steps.description: Replace flagged low-quality/low-specificity guides with better Brunello alternates where available; for genes short of 4 acceptable guides, note de-novo design need. Add control set: ~1,000 non-targeting guides (from Brunello), safe-harbor/intergenic cutting controls, and positive-control essential genes (e.g., RPL/RPS, POLR2A, proteasome) plus pan-essential kinases (CDK1, PLK1) for screen-quality benchmarking. Produce final_kinome_library.csv with a guide-type column.

steps.title: Library construction plan
steps.description: Design the synthesis-ready oligo pool: append 5' and 3' cloning adapters/BsmBI overhangs matching a standard lentiviral backbone (e.g., lentiGuide-Puro / lentiCRISPRv2), verify no internal BsmBI sites, and compute pool complexity. Specify cloning coverage targets (electroporation at >=300x representation), PCR amplification cycles, and Gibson/Golden-Gate ligation parameters. Produce oligo_pool_synthesis.csv and construction_protocol.md.

steps.title: Transduction and sequencing depth planning
steps.description: Compute the screen's scale parameters: cell numbers for maintaining >=500x guide representation at the target MOI (~0.3, accounting for puromycin survival), number of conditions x replicates x timepoints, and NGS read budget at >=300-1,000 reads/guide. Build a depth calculator covering readout PCR, sample multiplexing on a NovaSeq lane, and total reads/Gb required. Produce sequencing_depth_plan.csv and figure coverage_vs_cells.png.

steps.title: Compile design report
steps.description: Assemble a self-contained design report (design_report.html or .md) documenting: kinome definition and scope, guide-selection rationale and QC thresholds, off-target methodology and flagged guides, final library composition (targeting + controls), construction protocol, and sequencing depth math — with all figures embedded and every parameter stated. Include a README listing all deliverable files. Large raw tables referenced by name, not linked.

feasibility.rationale: Highly feasible. Core approach anchors on the published Brunello genome-wide library (4 optimized sgRNAs/gene, Azimuth Rule Set 2 + CFD) subset to the human protein kinome (~518 kinases, Manning/KinHub). The one step with moderate compute/download risk is genome-wide off-target re-analysis (requires the human genome FASTA + Cas-OFFinder), but this machine (128 CPU) handles it for the ~2,000-2,400 selected guides. Assumptions: human, SpCas9/NGG, 4 guides/gene; adjustable at approval.
